title: Circumvention research paper record
description: Schema for one paper in the circumvention research corpus. The controlled vocabularies for censors / techniques / defenses live in schema/taxonomy.yaml.

id.description: Stable id, format: YYYY-firstauthor-shortslug. Used as the YAML filename and as the MCP key.

venue.description: Publication venue (USENIX Security, NDSS, PETS, FOCI, IMC, arXiv, etc.) or 'preprint' / 'private communication'.

url.description: Canonical URL for the paper. For private papers, may be a local file:// or an internal URL.

pdf_path.description: Relative path under corpus/pdfs/ if a local copy is stored. Optional.

censors.description: Censoring entities the paper covers. Use IDs from schema/taxonomy.yaml censors.

techniques.description: Detection / blocking techniques the paper covers. Use IDs from taxonomy.

defenses_discussed.description: Circumvention techniques the paper mentions or analyses. Taxonomy IDs.

defenses_evaluated_against.description: Subset of defenses_discussed that the paper actively *evaluates* (rather than just citing).

core.description: Team-marked: should every Lantern researcher know about this paper? Used to prioritize daily reading and to weight search results.

notes.description: Free-form team notes. What made this important, what we should do about it, who should read it.

visibility.description: Visibility tier — see README. The public MCP endpoint serves only 'public' records.

embargo_until.description: Required when visibility=embargoed. ISO date when the paper becomes public.

redistribution_terms.description: Free-form description of the actual constraint. E.g. 'no external citation until FOCI 2026'. The visibility enum is the source of truth for automated filtering; this field is the source of truth for humans.

sources.description: Where we found this paper: arxiv:NNNN.NNNNN, net4people-issue:NNN, doi:..., 'manual', 'crawler:arxiv', etc.

references.description: Other paper IDs in this corpus that this paper cites or builds on.
